title: rosa sempervirens subsp. scandens
intro: Rosa sempervirens subsp. scandens, also known as the climbing rose or climbing wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa sempervirens plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa sempervirens subsp. scandens include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and reduce stress and anxiety.
